sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

itinerary.note: Gateway to Kodachrome Basin State Park
Junction toward Smokey Mountain Scenic Backway

paragraphs: The road from Bryce Canyon Junction to Tropic was added to the state highway system in 1914, and in 1923 a branch from Tropic Junction to Bryce Canyon was built as a forest road. The State Road Commission numbered the route to Bryce Canyon SR-12 in the 1920s, and in 1927 the state legislature assigned it to both branches, to Bryce Canyon and Tropic, but split off the branch to Tropic as State Route 54 in 1931. That same year, a new State Route 120 was created, continuing the road from Tropic to Henrieville, and in 1935 it became part of SR-54. Another road was also added to the state highway system in 1914, connecting SR-22 at Widtsoe with Escalante, and it was numbered State Route 23 in 1927. An extension took SR-23 northeast to Boulder in 1941, and in 1947 SR-54 absorbed SR-23, with the Widtsoe-Escalante road dropped in favor of Henrieville-Escalante. State Route 117, running southeast from SR-24 near Teasdale to Grover, became a state highway in 1931, and was extended south to Boulder in 1957, becoming part of SR-54 in 1966. In 1969, SR-54 became part of SR-12, but most of former SR-117 was dropped, leaving SR-12 to stretch from Bryce Canyon Junction to the north limit of Boulder (at the Dixie National Forest boundary), with a short spur to Bryce Canyon. This spur became a new SR-63 in 1975, and in 1985 the route was extended back north from Boulder to SR-24, using a different route than the old SR-117 north of Grover.

highlights.d: Gateway to Kodachrome Basin State Park

Escalante Canyon, Garfield County (UT) SR12 South
Utah Scenic Byway 12 winds through Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monument between Boulder and Escalate, Utah.
gallery.credit: Tony Webster from Minneapolis, Minnesota · CC BY-SA 2.0

gallery.cap: Scenic Byway 12 Autumn Aspen Dot the Mountainside
gallery.desc: The aspen on Boulder Mountain change color in September. Location: Boulder Mountain, Utah Status: Public domain. Photo by S. Clyde
Looking down from the Boynton Overlook, Scenic Byway 12 curves, and the Hogsback lays in the distance. Location: Utah Status: Public domain. Photo by Allysia Angus

gallery.cap: Scenic Byway 12 Breathtaking View from Scenic Byway 12 (UT)
gallery.desc: A serene landscape of sandstone stretches as far as the eye can see in the area near the Escalante River and Escalante Canyons of Utah. Location: Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monument, Utah (37.749° N 111.484° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Jessica Dungan

gallery.cap: Scenic Byway 12 Bruce Fullmer Speaks at Scenic Byway 12 All American R
gallery.desc: Bruce Fullmer of Garfield County says a few words at the Scenic Byway 12 All-American Road Designation event in Boulder, Utah. Location: Utah Status: Public domain. Photo by Dave Rogers of MDSC

gallery.cap: Scenic Byway 12 Byway Enthusiasts at Scenic Byway 12 Ribbon Cutting
gallery.desc: Nan Anderson, Bruce Fullmer, and many others gather in Boulder, Utah, to cut the ribbon across Scenic Byway 12, opening it for traffic as an All-American Road. Location: Utah Status: Public domain. Photo by Dave Rogers of MDSC

gallery.cap: Scenic Byway 12 Byway Panel in Escalante
gallery.desc: This is a close up of one of the interpretive panels at a byway roadside kiosk in Escalante. Location: Utah (37.770° N 111.600° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams

gallery.cap: Scenic Byway 12 Cattle Wander Freely Around Scenic Byway 12
gallery.desc: Parts of Scenic Byway 12 cross open range areas for livestock. Location: Utah Status: Public domain. Photo by S. Clyde

gallery.cap: Scenic Byway 12 Close Up of Anasazi Granary at "Intrigue of the Past"
gallery.desc: You'll need a pair of binoculars or a telephoto lens to get this close a look at this archaeological site. Location: Utah (37.911° N 111.423° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.desc: Scenic Byway 12 curves on its way to Panguitch. Location: Utah (37.765° N 112.850° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane

gallery.cap: Scenic Byway 12 Gold and Orange Aspen
gallery.desc: In the fall, on Scenic Byway 12, look for gold and orange aspen to match the gold and orange sandstone. Location: Utah Status: Public domain. Photo by S. Clyde
